title: Contributing to the ‘future.tests’ package
text: Git repository uses Git Flow branching model (git flow extension useful ). develop branch contains latest contributions code appear next release, master branch contains code latest release, exactly currently CRAN. Contributing package easy. Just send pull request. send PR, make sure develop destination branch future.tests repository. PR pass R CMD check ---cran, also checked GitHub Actions PR submitted. abide Code Conduct Contributor Covenant.

title: future.tests: An Introduction
text: future backends implementing Future API validate conform Future API. can done using future.tests package, provides two APIs running tests. tests can performed either within R outside R command line making easy include package tests Continuous Integration (CI) pipelines.

title: future.tests: An Introduction
code: > results <- future.tests::check(plan = "multisession") > exit_code <- attr(results, "exit_code") > if (exit_code != 0) stop("One or more tests failed")

title: future.tests: An Introduction
code: $ Rscript -e future.tests::check --test-plan="multisession" $ exit_code=$? $ [[ exit_code -eq 0 ]] || { >&2 echo "One or more tests failed"; exit 1; }

title: Authors and Citation
text: Henrik Bengtsson. Author, maintainer, copyright holder. R Consortium. Funder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Henrik Bengtsson, Unifying Framework Parallel Distributed Processing R using Futures, R Journal (2021) 13:2, pages 208-227, doi:10.32614/RJ-2021-048
code: @Article{RJ-2021-048,   author = {Henrik Bengtsson},   title = {A Unifying Framework for Parallel and Distributed Processing in R using Futures},   year = {2021},   journal = {The R Journal},   doi = {10.32614/RJ-2021-048},   url = {https://doi.org/10.32614/RJ-2021-048},   pages = {208--227},   volume = {13},   number = {2}, }

what: Test Suite for Future API Backends
title: Test Suite for Future API Backends
text: future package defines Future API consists small number functions writing R code can evaluated either sequentially parallel based single setting without change anything code. Parallelization can done via one many backends, e.g. via built-multicore, multisession cluster backends (based parallel package) via third-party backends future.batchtools, future.callr future.mirai. design motto Future API : Write , run anywhere order code work regardless future backend end-user chooses, critical backend fully complies Future API Backend Specification. future backend 100% compliance rate guarantees code work equally well sequential mode. R package - future.tests - provides test suite validation future backend complies Future API.

title: Test Suite for Future API Backends
text: future backends implementing Future API validate conform Future API. can done using future.tests package, provides two APIs running tests. tests can performed either within R outside R command line making easy include package tests Continuous Integration (CI) pipelines.

title: Test Suite for Future API Backends
code: > results <- future.tests::check(plan = "multisession") > exit_code <- attr(results, "exit_code") > if (exit_code != 0) stop("One or more tests failed")

title: Test Suite for Future API Backends
code: $ Rscript -e future.tests::check --test-plan="multisession" $ exit_code=$? $ [[ exit_code -eq 0 ]] || { >&2 echo "One or more tests failed"; exit 1; }

title: Test Suite for Future API Backends
text: R package future.tests available CRAN can installed R :
code: install.packages("future.tests")

title: Test Suite for Future API Backends
text: install pre-release version available Git branch develop GitHub, use: install package source.
code: remotes::install_github("futureverse/future.tests", ref="develop")

what: Add a Future Plan to Test Against — add_test_plan
title: Add a Future Plan to Test Against — add_test_plan
text: Add Future Plan Test

title: Add a Future Plan to Test Against — add_test_plan
code: add_test_plan(expr, substitute = TRUE)

title: Add a Future Plan to Test Against — add_test_plan
text: expr ... substitute ...

title: Add a Future Plan to Test Against — add_test_plan
text: (invisibly) returns current list test plans.

what: Evaluate an Expression Across A Set of Future Plans — along_test_plans
title: Evaluate an Expression Across A Set of Future Plans — along_test_plans
text: Evaluate Expression Across Set Future Plans

title: Evaluate an Expression Across A Set of Future Plans — along_test_plans
code: along_test_plans(   expr,   substitute = TRUE,   envir = parent.frame(),   local = TRUE,   plans = test_plans() )

title: Evaluate an Expression Across A Set of Future Plans — along_test_plans
text: expr R expression. substitute ... envir environment tests run. local tests evaluated local environment . plans list future plans.

title: Evaluate an Expression Across A Set of Future Plans — along_test_plans
text: list results, one future plan tested .

what: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
title: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
text: Run Subset Tests Across Future Plans

title: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
code: check(   plan = NULL,   tags = character(),   timeout = NULL,   settings = TRUE,   session_info = FALSE,   envir = parent.frame(),   local = TRUE,   debug = FALSE,   exit_value = !interactive(),   .args = commandArgs() )

title: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
text: plan (character vector) One future strategy plans validated. tags (character vector; optional) Filter tests tags. NULL, tests performed. timeout (numeric; optional) Maximum time (seconds) test may run timeout error produced. settings (logical) TRUE, details settings outputted tests start. session_info (logical) TRUE, session information outputted tests complete. envir environment tests run. local tests evaluated local environment . debug (logical) TRUE, raw test results printed. exit_value (logical) TRUE, non-interactive session, use base::quit() quit R exit code 0 (zero) tests passed OKs otherwise 1 (one) one tests failed. .args (character vector; optional) Command-line arguments.

title: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
text: (list; invisible) list test results.

title: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
text: function can called shell. specify argument, use format --test-<arg_name>=<value>.  example, --test-timeout=600 set argument timeout=600, --tags=lazy,rng, equivalently, --tags=lazy --tags=rng set argument tags=c("lazy", "rng"). examples call function command line: exit code 0 tests passed, otherwise 1. can use instance exit_code=$? retrieve exit code recent call.
code: Rscript -e future.tests::check --args --test-plan=sequential Rscript -e future.tests::check --args --test-plan=multicore,workers=2 Rscript -e future.tests::check --args --test-plan=sequential --test-plan=multicore,workers=2 Rscript -e future.tests::check --args --test-plan=future.callr::callr Rscript -e future.tests::check --args --test-plan=future.batchtools::batchtools_local

title: Run All or a Subset of the Tests Across Future Plans — check
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- future.tests::check(plan = "sequential", tags = c("rng")) exit_code <- attr(results, "exit_code") if (exit_code != 0) stop("One or more tests failed") } # }

title: Run All Tests — check_plan
text: tests list tests subset. defaults (optional) Named list default argument values. timeout Maximum time allowed evaluation timeout error produced. envir environment tests run. local tests evaluated local environment .

title: Run All Tests — check_plan
text: Nothing.

text: Evaluate R Expression

text: expr expression envir environment tests run. local tests evaluated local environment . output Specifies whether standard output, standard error, captured . timeout Maximum time allowed evaluation timeout error produced.

title: Loads Future Tests — load_tests
text: Loads Future Tests

title: Loads Future Tests — load_tests
code: load_tests(   path = ".",   recursive = TRUE,   pattern = "[.]R$",   root = getOption("future.tests.root", Sys.getenv("R_FUTURE_TESTS_ROOT",     system.file("test-db", package = "future.tests", mustWork = TRUE))) )

title: Loads Future Tests — load_tests
text: path character string specifying test script folder file. recursive TRUE, test-definition scripts searched recursively. pattern Regular expression matching filenames include. root (internal) alternative file directory future.tests tests sourced.

title: Loads Future Tests — load_tests
text: (invisible) value test_db().

title: Make a Test — make_test
text: Make Test

title: Make a Test — make_test
code: make_test(   expr,   title = NA_character_,   args = list(),   tags = NULL,   substitute = TRUE,   reset_workers = FALSE,   register = !("devel" %in% tags) || isTRUE(getOption("future.tests.devel")) )

title: Make a Test — make_test
text: expr, substitute expression tested whether passed expression already . title (character) title test. args (optional) Named arguments. tags (optional) Character vector tags. reset_workers (optional) Specifies whether background workers futures. register TRUE, test registered test database, otherwise . default register test, unless tags contains "devel".

title: Make a Test — make_test
text: (invisibly) Test.

title: Version 1.0.0
text: Changed package license permissive Apache License (>= 2).

title: Version 1.0.0
text: Add logical R option future.tests.devel, can set environment variable R_FUTURE_TESTS_DEVEL. controls whether tests "devel" (“development”) tag included. check() reports future version.

title: Version 1.0.0
text: Add tests cancel(). Assert plan() accepts argument interrupts. Remove tests future(..., earlySignal = FALSE/TRUE), deprecated future package. Relax assertions test resolved() lazy futures.

title: Version 1.0.0
text: Tests plan() adjusting argument workers skipped plan tested already sets argument workers fixed value, e.g. plan(cluster, workers = cl).

title: Version 0.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-04-15

title: Version 0.9.0
text: Now test code displayed check error.

title: Version 0.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-03-31

title: Version 0.8.0
text: Now load_tests() returns named list, names corresponds test titles. Now future.tests::check("mirai::daemons(2); plan(future.mirai::mirai_cluster)") works.

title: Version 0.8.0
text: Remove plan() test legacy constant backend.

title: Version 0.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-22

title: Version 0.7.0
text: Tests asserting correctness nbrOfWorkers() produce “invalid format ‘%d’; use format %f, %e, %g %numeric objects” error trying produce assertion error nbrOfWorkers() incorrect value. happen nbrOfWorkers() returned non-integer value, e.g. +Inf. Test asserting workers argument can function always work testing hardcoded number workers according plan(). Test asserting workers argument can function work backend’s default value non-numeric, e.g. cluster backend defaults character vector parallelly::availableWorkers(). Test asserting lazy futures automatically launched resolved relied legacy version Future API, calling resolved() lazy future leave lazy state, longer correct. lazy future always launched one calls resolved() . Test asserting ff package worked across multiple futures assumed package loaded automatically future, . cause test fail future backends.

title: Version 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-11

title: Version 0.6.0
text: Now tests can formally skipped calling future.tests::skip_test() within test. Skipped tests counted reported summary. Now check_plan() outputs reason test skipped. Now check_plan() outputs also error message, error class, call, standard output, whenever test error. Now check_plan() outputs also test iteration index. Added Rscript -e future.tests::check --version.

title: Version 0.6.0
text: tests assume future strategy tested workers argument, true future backends. example, ‘sequential’ take argument workers. Previously, avoided problem testing evaluator inherited multiprocess, sufficient, e.g. upcoming future.redis::redis inherits multiprocess, still workers argument. Now check workers argument instead.

title: Version 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-12-16

title: Version 0.5.0
text: Assert none future(), run(), result() value() update RNG state.

title: Version 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-22

title: Version 0.4.0
text: Assert rm() future expression removes local variable , never global variable . Assert packages data.table ff affected future resets R options worker resolved. Assert global copy non-exported package object (e.g. utils:::str2logical()) dropped belongs package namespace. Assert ... can exported global future, used -inside function ... arguments.

title: Version 0.4.0
text: check() check_plan() gained argument local, passed run_test(). check() gained argument envir, passed run_test().

title: Version 0.4.0
text: TestResult class record whether test evaluated local environment . strict internal assertion give Error evaluate_expr(test$expr, envir = envir, local = FALSE, output = output, : identical(Sys.getenv(), old$envvars) TRUE R 4.2.x R-devel MS Windows. possible remove environment variables MS Windows; can set empty value.

title: Version 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-10-10

title: Version 0.3.0
text: Assert future() doesn’t change RNG kind. Assert future(..., conditions = character(0L)) muffles conditions.

title: Version 0.3.0
text: robustness, using explicit stringsAsFactors = FALSE internally. evaluate_expr(), used running tests, now reset options, environment variables, RNG kind, random seed afterward called. Added package vignettes.

title: Version 0.3.0
text: Tests resolve() use deprecated argument value.

title: Version 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2020-03-20

title: Version 0.2.1
text: Update package description use single quotes. Add example check().

title: Version 0.2.0
text: First version released CRAN.

title: Version 0.1.1
text: Assert resolved() launch lazy futures.

title: Version 0.1.0
text: non-interactive mode, check() quit R exit code reflects whether tests passed (0) (1). check() gained arguments can easily called R .

title: Version 0.0.0-9000
text: Created package stub.
